content: We can model healthy socio-emotional development for a firstborn child ("child1") after the LLM Self-Refine framework, framing a child’s early growth as an unsupervised iterative self-refinement process that requires no pre-scripted "training curricula" from caregivers. Just as Self-Refine lets LLMs improve their outputs through repeated self-feedback without external labeled data, a firstborn child can build adaptive social and problem-solving skills by self-critiquing their unprompted exploratory actions, rather than only following adult instruction.
